text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on but with a real headwind - Cleveland Fed's Hammack floated rate HIKES if AI fuels inflation. That's the single biggest threat to your book today. Tech and chips are rate-sensitive, and you're on margin, so this isn't background noise.

Your holdings: PTNM is down 15.25% and is a tiny spec with thin data - on margin, this kind of name can gap through your $9.50 stop fast, so size matters. SOXL is a 3x leveraged semiconductor ETF - it's up 4.13% but decays daily and will get hit hard if rate-hike talk gains traction. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history - margin amplifies any sudden drop here, manage it manually and keep size small.

Watch: Fed speakers this week - any pile-on to Hammack's rate-hike language would pressure your entire tech/chip book hard. Also watch SMH as your chip-sector thermometer.

Bottom line: Protect what you have - the rate-hike headline is the real risk to your concentrated, leveraged tech book today.
plays: PTNM: Down 15.25% and it's a spec with thin data on margin - if it breaks $9.50, exit without hesitation; no reason to add here.
SOXL: Up 4.13% but 3x leveraged decay plus rate-hike talk is a bad combo - consider trimming to reduce margin exposure if Fed rhetoric hardens.
No new buys today - scanner setups for AMAT and KLAC look decent technically but buying into chip premium while rate-hike risk is live is not the right moment.
